name: Root
description: Contract specifications for cross-cutting concerns in OpenClaw Mission Control. Defines pure TypeScript type definitions and protocols (Result, serialization, events, aggregations, errors, naming conventions) that subsystems must conform to. Zero runtime code—purely types and interfaces. No dependencies; all subsystems reference root for conformance contracts.

types.name: ErrorKind
types.description: Standard error categories for all subsystems. Finite set of error types for discriminated error handling.

types.name: StandardError

fields.name: kind
fields.description: Error category discriminant

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message

validators.error_message: Error message must be between 1 and 10000 characters

fields.name: context
fields.description: Additional structured error context (field names, paths, codes, etc.)

fields.name: cause
fields.description: Underlying error message or stack trace from source system

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when error occurred

validators.error_message: timestamp must be valid ISO 8601 format

types.description: Standard error interface that all subsystems implement. Enables uniform error handling and reporting across the application.

types.name: ResultOk

fields.name: ok
fields.description: Literal true for success variant

validators.error_message: ok must be true for ResultOk

fields.name: value
fields.description: The success payload. Actual type depends on function signature (generic T).

types.description: Success variant of Result discriminated union

types.name: ResultErr

fields.name: ok
fields.description: Literal false for error variant

validators.error_message: ok must be false for ResultErr

fields.name: error
fields.description: The structured error value

types.description: Error variant of Result discriminated union

types.name: Result
types.description: Result type protocol for all fallible operations. Discriminated union of ResultOk | ResultErr. All data access functions must return Result<T, StandardError>.

types.name: ISODateString

fields.name: value
fields.description: ISO 8601 formatted timestamp with timezone (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ or +HH:mm)

types.description: ISO 8601 timestamp string type. All date/time values must use this format (no Date objects across serialization boundaries).

types.name: Serializable

fields.name: toJSON
fields.description: Signature of toJSON method: () => Record<string, JSONValue>. Implementers must provide this method that returns a JSON-serializable object with no circular references, Functions, or Symbols.

types.description: Protocol contract for types that can be serialized to JSON/NDJSON. All SSE event payloads and API responses must implement this contract.

types.name: Deserializable

fields.name: fromJSON
fields.description: Signature of static fromJSON method: (data: Record<string, JSONValue>) => Result<T, StandardError>. Implementers must validate via Zod before constructing instance.

types.description: Protocol contract for types that can be deserialized from JSON. All persisted events and API request bodies must implement this contract.

types.name: EventVersion
types.description: Event schema version discriminator for forward compatibility

types.name: EventEnvelope

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique event identifier (UUID v4 or nanoid). Used for SSE Last-Event-ID and deduplication.

validators.error_message: Must be nanoid (21+ chars) or UUID v4

fields.name: type
fields.description: Dot-notation event type (e.g., 'agent.started', 'pact.transition'). Used for filtering and routing.

validators.error_message: Must be dot-notation lowercase event type

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when event occurred

fields.name: version
fields.description: Event schema version (always v1 for current schema)

fields.name: payload
fields.description: Event-specific data conforming to event type schema. Must be JSON-serializable.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Optional envelope-level metadata (trace IDs, correlation IDs, source system)

types.description: Standard envelope for all SSE and persisted events. Subsystems must wrap event payloads in this envelope. Enables versioning, ordering, and deduplication.

types.name: TimeBucketGranularity
types.description: Time bucket granularities for aggregation queries

types.name: AggregationDimension
types.description: Standard dimensions for grouping aggregations (cost, metrics, activity)

types.name: AggregateQuery

fields.name: dimensions
fields.description: List of AggregationDimension values to group by

fields.name: time_bucket
fields.description: Time bucket size when 'time_bucket' is in dimensions

fields.name: period_start
fields.description: ISO 8601 start of query period

fields.name: period_end
fields.description: ISO 8601 end of query period

fields.name: filters
fields.description: Optional dimension filters (e.g., {agent_id: 'agent-1', project_id: 'proj-2'})

types.description: Protocol contract for aggregate query parameters. Subsystems use this for cost/metrics/activity aggregation queries.

types.name: FilesystemErrorCode
types.description: Standard filesystem error codes mapped from Node.js ENOENT, EACCES, etc. Used by data layer for filesystem error handling.

types.name: FilesystemError

fields.name: code
fields.description: Filesystem error code

fields.name: path
fields.description: File or directory path that caused the error

fields.name: operation
fields.description: Filesystem operation that failed (read, write, readdir, stat, etc.)

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message

fields.name: syscall
fields.description: System call that failed (open, read, stat, etc.)

types.description: Structured filesystem error with path context. Data layer maps Node.js fs errors to this type.

types.name: FixtureData

fields.name: data
fields.description: The fixture data payload. Actual type depends on fixture type (generic T).

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Fixture metadata (description, version, created_at, tags)

types.description: Generic wrapper for test fixture data. Testing subsystem uses this to type fixture objects.

types.name: NamingConventionRule
types.description: Naming convention rules enforced across the codebase. Documented in JSDoc, validated in linting.

functions.name: createResultOk
functions.description: Pure factory function to construct a ResultOk value. Used by subsystems to create success results. No validation—caller must ensure value is valid.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The success payload. Generic type T depends on caller context.

functions.name: createResultErr
functions.description: Pure factory function to construct a ResultErr value. Used by subsystems to create error results. Validates that error conforms to StandardError shape via runtime check.

error_cases.name: InvalidErrorStructure
error_cases.condition: error does not conform to StandardError shape

error_data.message: Error must conform to StandardError interface

preconditions: error.kind must be valid ErrorKind variant

functions.name: isResultOk
functions.description: Pure type guard to check if Result is the Ok variant. Used for discriminated union narrowing in subsystems.

functions.name: isResultErr
functions.description: Pure type guard to check if Result is the Err variant. Used for discriminated union narrowing in subsystems.

functions.name: toISODateString
functions.description: Pure converter from Date object or timestamp number to ISO 8601 string. Used by subsystems to ensure all date/time values are serialized consistently. No Date objects across serialization boundaries.

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Date object, Unix timestamp (ms), or ISO 8601 string. If already ISO string, returns as-is after validation.

error_cases.name: InvalidDateInput
error_cases.condition: Input is not a Date, number, or parseable ISO string

error_data.message: Input must be Date, Unix timestamp, or ISO 8601 string

error_cases.name: InvalidDate
error_cases.condition: Input Date object is Invalid Date

preconditions: If input is number, must be valid Unix timestamp in milliseconds
If input is Date, must be valid Date object (not Invalid Date)
If input is string, must be parseable as ISO 8601

functions.name: fromISODateString
functions.description: Pure parser from ISO 8601 string to Date object. Used when subsystems need to perform date arithmetic (not for serialization). Returns Result<Date, StandardError>.

error_cases.name: ParseError
error_cases.condition: isoString cannot be parsed into valid Date (malformed, out-of-range values)

error_data.message: Failed to parse ISO 8601 string into Date

postconditions: On success, result.value is valid Date object with time matching isoString

functions.name: createEventEnvelope
functions.description: Pure factory to construct an EventEnvelope from event type, payload, and optional metadata. Generates unique ID (nanoid) and current timestamp. Used by subsystems when emitting events to SSE bus.

validators.error_message: Must be dot-notation lowercase event type

inputs.name: payload
inputs.description: Event-specific data conforming to event type schema. Must be JSON-serializable.

error_cases.name: InvalidEventType
error_cases.condition: type does not match dot-notation regex

error_data.message: Event type must be dot-notation lowercase

error_cases.name: NonSerializablePayload
error_cases.condition: payload contains non-serializable values (circular refs, Functions, Symbols)

preconditions: payload must be JSON-serializable (no circular refs, Functions, Symbols)
postconditions: Returned envelope.timestamp is current time as ISO 8601 string

functions.name: mapNodeFsError
functions.description: Pure mapper from Node.js fs error to FilesystemError. Used by data layer to convert Node.js errors to structured domain errors. Extracts code, path, syscall from Node error object.

inputs.name: nodeError
inputs.description: Node.js fs error object (typically has code, path, syscall properties)

inputs.name: operation
inputs.description: Filesystem operation being attempted (read, write, readdir, stat, etc.)

postconditions: Returned error.code is mapped from nodeError.code to FilesystemErrorCode enum (defaults to UNKNOWN if unrecognized)
Returned error.path is nodeError.path if present, otherwise empty string
Returned error.syscall is nodeError.syscall if present

functions.name: createStandardError
functions.description: Pure factory to construct a StandardError from kind, message, and optional context/cause. Automatically sets timestamp to current time as ISO 8601 string. Used by all subsystems to create uniform errors.

inputs.name: cause
inputs.description: Underlying error message or stack trace

error_cases.name: InvalidMessage
error_cases.condition: message is empty or exceeds 10000 characters

postconditions: Returned error.context === input context (or {} if omitted)
Returned error.cause === input cause (or empty string if omitted)
Returned error.timestamp is current time as ISO 8601 string

functions.name: validateNamingConvention
functions.description: Pure validator to check if a name adheres to a NamingConventionRule. Used by linting tools and code generation utilities. Returns Result<bool, StandardError> where value=true means valid, error variant means invalid with details.

inputs.name: rule
inputs.description: The naming convention rule to apply

error_cases.name: InvalidFunctionName
error_cases.condition: rule is FUNCTION_NAMES_CAMEL_CASE and name does not match /^[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$/

error_cases.name: InvalidTypeName
error_cases.condition: rule is TYPE_NAMES_PASCAL_CASE and name does not match /^[A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$/

error_cases.name: InvalidEnumVariant
error_cases.condition: rule is ENUM_VARIANTS_SNAKE_CASE and name does not match /^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/

error_cases.name: InvalidComponentId
error_cases.condition: rule is COMPONENT_IDS_SNAKE_CASE and name does not match /^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/

error_cases.name: InvalidEventType
error_cases.condition: rule is EVENT_TYPES_DOT_NOTATION_LOWERCASE and name does not match /^[a-z]+([.][a-z_]+)+$/

error_cases.name: InvalidApiRoute
error_cases.condition: rule is API_ROUTES_KEBAB_CASE and name does not match /^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$/

error_cases.name: InvalidDatabaseColumn
error_cases.condition: rule is DATABASE_COLUMNS_SNAKE_CASE and name does not match /^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/

error_cases.name: InvalidBrandedType
error_cases.condition: rule is BRANDED_TYPES_SUFFIX_ID and name does not match /[A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9]*Id$/

error_data.message: Branded ID types must be PascalCase ending with 'Id'

postconditions: On success, result.value === true if name matches rule pattern
On error, result.error.kind === 'ValidationError' with message explaining violation

invariants: All Result values have exactly one of ok=true with value OR ok=false with error (never both, never neither)
All StandardError.timestamp values are valid ISO 8601 strings with timezone
All EventEnvelope.id values are globally unique (enforced by nanoid/uuid collision resistance)
All EventEnvelope.version values equal 'v1' for current schema
All FilesystemError.code values are valid FilesystemErrorCode enum variants
All AggregateQuery.period_end values are >= period_start (chronological ordering)
All Serializable implementers must produce JSON-serializable output (no circular refs, Functions, Symbols)
All Deserializable implementers must validate via Zod before constructing instances
All subsystems return Result<T, StandardError> for fallible operations (never throw exceptions for expected errors)
All event payloads are JSON-serializable (validated in createEventEnvelope)
All naming convention rules are documented in JSDoc and enforced via validateNamingConvention
Root has zero dependencies (purely type definitions and protocols)
Root exports no runtime code (only TypeScript types and type guards)
